summary: current: MT2 passes: dimensions and tile codes are valid, exact MT2 resource quotas are met, tracked tools stay within remaining whole-tower budget, enemies obey current_floor_policy with no orthogonal adjacency, and the map provides plausible left, center, and right pressured routes with protected rewards. | connectivity density: Connectivity and density pass: wall_ratio 0.592 and main_cycle_rank_ratio 0.072 indicate a compact, controlled layout rather than an open grid; monster_density_non_wall 0.232 is suitably high; the minimum exit route has 4 enemies and 2 doors, and the graph supports controlled center, left, and right candidate routes. | resource balance: Resource access is staged behind monsters and doors, the only low-cost cluster bbox [3,8]->[6,12] contains just a yellow-key refund at [4,9], blue doors at [7,10] and [9,6] unlock stronger reward/route packages, and the pickaxe at [11,10] is protected by blue/yellow door pressure plus combat. | monster damage: Monster pacing is acceptable: the min route uses skeletons at [6,11], [3,7], [5,2] plus zombie [3,4] as real costs, while optional center/right branches use redPriest, bigBat, and slimelord placements to guard stronger rewards. No zone/repulse sources are present, so there is no decorative or uncompensated zone/repulse damage. | gem route balance: Gem route balance reviewer found 4 route/resource imbalance issue(s).
